channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple successfully blocks Match from data in India antitrust case
item.description: Apple has successfully blocked its opponents in India, Tinder-owner Match and a group of startups, from accessing its commercially sensitive information which was part of antitrust findings against the U.S. firm, a confidential order shows.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Moonvalley launches an AI video generator built for moviemaking
item.description: A well-funded AI lab with a deep bench of research talent is releasing a powerful new model that generates high-definition video for the film and advertising industries.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: NASA’s new AI satellites could revolutionize disaster response
item.description: Satellite-based disaster monitoring has been a slow and tedious process for decades. The process consists of capturing images, transmitting them back to Earth, and relying on human analysts to interpret the data.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: Exploring DORA: 9 steps on the path to compliance
item.description: The Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) is a significant regulatory framework introduced by the European Union to enhance the operational resilience of digital systems within the financial sector. The regulations went into effect on January 17, 2025.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FTC asks judge to delay Amazon trial due to resource constraints
item.description: HALELUYA HADERO The Federal Trade Commission asked a federal judge on Wednesday to delay a trial in a case accusing Amazon of using deceptive practices in its Prime subscription program, citing staffing and budgetary challenges at the government agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: GTA Online publisher Take-Two is gunning for a black market that’s basically heaven for cheaters
item.description: Take-Two is going after online marketplace PlayerAuctionsfor allegedly selling hacked GTA Online accounts and currency.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 6 in 10 say Musk lacks experience, judgment to change government: Survey
item.description: Elon Musk, a key political ally of President Trump, is viewed negatively by most Americans and they question his qualifications to overhaul the federal government, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Alibaba releases AI model that reads emotions to take on OpenAI
item.description: Alibaba’s effort to carve out a leading position in AI was accelerated by DeepSeek’s splashy debut in January.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Aptori Now on Google Cloud Marketplace for AI-Powered Security and Automated Risk Remediation
item.description: Aptori’s AI-Driven AppSec Platform Proactively Eliminates Vulnerabilities to Minimize Risk and Ensure Compliance.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French Publishers Take Meta to Court Over AI Copyright Issues
item.description: France's publishing industry takes on Meta over alleged copyright infringement in training its generative AI model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the government study trying to understand the health effects of ultraprocessed foods
item.description: A National Institutes of Health study aims to find out whether ultraprocessed foods lead people to eat more.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Con el pensamiento crítico en declive, TI debe replantearse la usabilidad de las aplicaciones
item.description: La usabilidad en el diseño de aplicaciones ha significado históricamente ofrecer un diseño de interfaz intuitivo que facilite a los usuarios objetivo la navegación y el trabajo eficaz con un sistema.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: You season 5: everything we know so far about the twisted Netflix show’s return
item.description: You season 5 is coming on April 24 – here's everything you need to know about the hit Netflix thriller before it's released.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How science is individualizing pregnancy risks
item.description: Lindsay Orr was active and healthy, running marathons and hiking all around Colorado. During pregnancy, she developed a persistent headache and dangerously high blood pressure—hallmark symptoms of preeclampsia, a leading cause of preterm birth as well as maternal mortality and morbidity.
item.dc:creator: Maneesh Jain, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IBM wins UK lawsuit against LzLabs for mainframe intellectual property theft
item.description: When it comes to protecting its mainframe technology, IBM wields a pretty big sword. This week, its defense triumphed as IBM won a judgement against LzLabs for violating Big Blue’s intellectual property rights.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk says X outages caused by cyberattack
item.description: Musk hypothesized on his platform that "Either a large, coordinated group and/or a country" were responsible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Protect Your Privacy with Apple's App Privacy Report
item.description: Learn how to use Apple's App Privacy Report to monitor app behavior, protect your personal information, and make informed decisions about your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Building generative AI? Get ready for generative UI
item.description: There’s currently no stopping generative AI spending. Analyst firm Omdia expects spending on generative AI applications to hit $58 billion by 2028. Yet while this level of investment is a significant amount, actual deployment of generative AI applications is still slow.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 things developers love about JavaScript – and 10 things they don’t
item.description: It was 30 years ago today, Sgt. JavaScript taught the web to play. Well, not exactly but close enough. The programming language began in 1995 as a project in the depths of the browser maker Netscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How genomics and AI could help treat cancer more effectively
item.description: “These discoveries highlight how genetic profiling can play a crucial role in personalizing cancer care,” Ruishan Liu said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Got a bank transfer alert text? It might be a scam; here’s what to do
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to safeguard your personal information as scammers become more sophisticated with scam texts and other methods.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Helping DOGE cut waste
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At the tactical edge, Army wants 58 zero trust capabilities
item.description: CWO Four Ben Koontz, the director of the Army’s functional management office for zero trust, said a recent pilot successfully tested these cyber tools. The post At the tactical edge, Army wants 58 zero trust capabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft reportedly struggling to build its own reasoning models to rival OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft may be “all in” with AI, but its efforts to produce homegrown products to feed its ambitions have so far been lagging and its reliance on OpenAI prevails. However, recent events may augur change.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Stop carrying your physical driver's license and add it to your Apple Wallet
item.description: A digital ID may be even more secure than a physical ID. Storing your driver's license in your Apple Wallet is an ideal solution for commuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
